fields.title: Memory index + read-by-id redesign
fields.description: Tau memory currently injects full stored content into the system prompt and stores only a single content field per entry. We want the best parts of tau and Claude memory: keep canonical JSONL as the only source of truth, enrich entries with indexable metadata, inject only a compact memory index into the prompt, and let the model read full memory on demand through the memory tool instead of via derived files.
fields.design: Keep canonical storage in the existing JSONL buckets only. Extend entries to carry explicit scope/type plus a short summary and full content. Preserve ids, timestamps, locking, and frozen-snapshot semantics. Replace full-memory prompt injection with a compact <memory_index> view containing id, scope, type, and summary only. Add a memory read action that resolves an id from canonical JSONL and returns a rendered full entry. Do not introduce derived per-entry files or any second source of truth.
fields.notes: Implementation split: deep handles storage/schema/index contract; smart handles tool/prompt/renderer integration; final pass uses review agent.
